author: nashatyrev
content:
From my understanding the peer scoring is mostly up to implementation. If a client is too restrictive then it would likely struggle finding and maintaining a pool of peers so this is kind of self-regulating. 
Not well aware of the Prysm implementation in particular, but the logic could be to downscore  offline nodes to reduce the number of further reconnect attempts. If it prevents inbound connections from this node, then it's likely a bug.
